name: Galaxy Cats
notes: Planet scene: The scene where you can view and tap on the planets.
Star scene: The scene where you can catch shooting stars.
Meteor scene: The scene where you can catch meteors.
Game scene: The on-planet scene where the different treehouses and cats appear

tests.action: Rotate from portrait to landscape and back again.
expected: The app does not change orientation or crash.

preconditions: The app is open and progress has been made (resources collected, planets unlocked, cats visited).
tests.action: Completely close and reopen the app.
expected: Progress has been saved.

preconditions: The app is not currently open.
expected: The 'Box Cat Games' splash screen shows for a few seconds.
The splash screen fades into the Planet scene, which shows the 'Galaxy Cats' name at the top for a few seconds.
The Planet scene shows the 'Galaxy Cats' name only on first load.

tests.action: View the scene. Tap on the screen if the controls are hidden.
expected: The resource counters (stars and meteors) are visible in every scene.
The resource counters are hidden in Chill Mode (accessible from Settings)

tests.action: Don't tap anything for a few seconds (around 10).
expected: The controls should fade out.
Tapping the screen anywhere brings the controls back.

preconditions: The user is on a Star or Meteor-catching scene or on the Game scene, ready to purchase from the in-game store.
tests.action: Take action to change the resource counts. Catch a star or meteor or make a purchase from the in-game store.
expected: The associated resource counter updates immediately.

tests.action: View the scene. Tap on the screen if the controls are hidden.
expected: The Planet scene allows the user to go to the star and meteor-catching scenes.
The Planet scene allows the user to collect the daily rewards.
The Planet scene allows the user to access the settings.
The Star and Meteor-catching scenes allow the user to go back.
The Game scene allows the user to go to the Star, Meteor, or Planet scenes.
The Game scene allows the user to collect the daily rewards.
The Game scene allows the user to view the list of cats for that planet.
The Game scene allows the user to access the settings.
The Game scene allows the user to view the store for that planet.
The settings should appear.
The Space or Meteor scene should load in Chill Mode (no shooting stars/meteors).
The left-to-right setting changes the direction of the shooting stars and meteors.
The music, SFX/audio, and planet-effects settings take immediate effect.
The slow-mode setting slows the velocity of the shooting stars and meteors.
The low-animation settings take immediate effect.
The low-animation settings stop the twinkling of the background stars, resource-counter badge updates, etc.
The app version is visible.
There is no 'Tech Zone' or mention of 'Dev Mode' on the settings overlay.

preconditions: At least one variant of each item in each of the planets' stores have been purchased.
Between 1-3 planets are unlocked/visible on the Planet scene.
expected: A new planet is visible and can be visited/tapped.
There are a total of 4 planets to unlock.
The store for that planet is visible and scrollable.
If an item has variants the user can scroll through them.
The prices for items are visible.
The currently in-use variant can be switched with a new purchase or a previously purchased variant.

preconditions: On the Game scene with the Store open.
tests.action: Choose an unpurchased variant for an item and click the purchase button.
expected: The item should be visible on the Game scene before purchase.

preconditions: On the Game scene with the Store open.
tests.action: Choose an unpurchased variant for an item and click the purchase button.
expected: After the preview the purchase can be completed or canceled.
If purchasing an item, the star count should decrease by the price.
